purpose: Next-phase implementation roadmap for Zeptalytic Web Backend transactional email service implementation using Brevo.
strategy: Use the committed transactional email architecture/control docs first, then generate exactly one focused implementation spec for Brevo-backed transactional email infrastructure, followed by planning and one-item-at-a-time build iterations.

specs.goal: Implement Brevo-backed transactional email infrastructure for the Zeptalytic Web Backend, including configuration, template catalog, sender profile resolver, Brevo client abstraction, provider-neutral EmailService, send-attempt logging, delivery-event webhook ingestion, and approved auth/account email flows.
specs.notes: Keep this implementation spec backend-only and focused. Do not edit the frontend repo. Do not edit the Pay Service repo. Do not invent billing, newsletter, or support workflow email triggers. Do not implement retry/outbox workers unless a later spec explicitly scopes them.

expected_implementation_sequence: template catalog and sender profile resolver
auth integration for forgot-password/password reset email
account-details-changed notification only where existing reset/account flow safely supports it
Brevo webhook route with secret validation
tests for configuration, template mapping, sender mapping, Brevo client behavior, send-attempt logging, auth integration, webhook ingestion, deduplication, and failure handling
full backend compile and Docker test verification

first_phase_email_triggers.trigger: only where existing password reset or account detail change flow safely supports it

configured_future_scope_templates.future_scope: internal/security use only unless explicitly approved
email-change flow only if a safe existing flow exists or later spec creates one

global_guardrails: Search before editing.
Do not expand scope beyond the active spec.
Do not add dependencies unless John explicitly approves them.
Do not edit the frontend repo.
Do not create frontend API clients.
Do not modify React/Vite files.
Do not redesign stable frontend pages.
Do not edit the Pay Service repo.
Do not duplicate Pay commercial business rules in parent.
Do not store sensitive payment details in parent.
Do not invent billing/order/payment email triggers.
Do not invent newsletter/update email triggers.
Do not invent support workflow email triggers.
Do not invent an account email-change workflow if one does not already exist.
Do not build admin dashboards unless explicitly scoped.
Do not implement automatic retry/outbox worker in this phase.
Do not use no-reply@zeptalytic.com.
Use real reply-capable senders.
Preserve HTTP-only cookie auth.
Preserve pending-verification access restrictions.
Signup must succeed even if verification email sending fails.
Forgot-password must remain account-enumeration safe.
Welcome email must be sent after successful email verification.
Welcome email failure must not undo successful verification.
Do not verify accounts based on Brevo sent/delivered/opened/clicked events.
Do not mutate account, billing, Pay, or support-ticket state from email delivery events.
Do not store raw verification tokens.
Do not store raw password reset tokens.
Do not store full token URLs in operational metadata.
Do not store rendered email bodies.
Do not commit real Brevo API keys.
Do not commit real webhook secrets.
Progress entries must be appended at EOF.
Do not mark incomplete items complete.
python -m compileall app tests alembic must pass before marking implementation items complete.
docker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml up --build --abort-on-container-exit --exit-code-from test must pass before marking implementation items complete.
